input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Agent role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

Pipeline position: after summary1 and before second-hop retrieval. Inputs are the original question and the first-hop intermediate state. Functional responsibility: produce one focused retrieval query that is likely to recover the missing evidence needed by the downstream agents. Query quality is determined by evidence recovery, not by whether the query itself resembles or contains the final answer.

Native output contract: The module must consume `question` and `summary_1` and produce exactly one compact second-hop query field named `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include every distinct entity token visible in the inputs in the single terse query and place at most one neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the targeted relation or attribute (for example: "EntityA EntityB director").
- If a visible disambiguator or attribute token appears adjacent to an entity in the inputs (for example a multiword service name, an explicit page title, a year, or an attribute marker like a birth/producer/service/series cue), include that disambiguator token together with the associated entity in the query rather than dropping or truncating it.
- If `summary_1` contains an explicit page-title token sequence (for example an instruction-like ExactPageTitle or Fetch page title), prefer that exact page-title token sequence as the query value.
- When the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Bertrand Tavernier | Bertrand Tavernier (born 25 April 1941) is a French director, screenwriter, actor and producer.
Safe Conduct | Safe Conduct (French: Laissez-passer ) is a 2002 French historical drama film directed by Bertrand Tavernier and written by Tavernier and Jean Cosmos.

Holy Lola | Holy Lola is a 2004 French drama film that is directed by Bertrand Tavernier. Tavernier said that the film was very, very moving, very exciting to do, and it made him fall in love with Cambodia.

Beatrice (film) | Beatrice (French:La passion Béatrice, Italian:Quarto comandamento) is a 1987 French-Italian historical drama film directed by Bertrand Tavernier and starring Bernard-Pierre Donnadieu, Julie Delpy and Nils Tavernier.

Dans la Nuit (Louis Sclavis album) | Dans la Nuit is an album by French clarinetist Louis Sclavis recorded in 2000 and released on the ECM label. The album was initialised through an invitation of French director Bertrand Tavernier to Sclavis, he agreed and wrote this music to Charles Vanel’s 1929 film “Dans la nuit”.

Nils Tavernier | Nils Tavernier (born 1 September 1965) is a French actor and director. He is best known for his film appearances in "Beatrice" (1987), "Valmont" (1988), and "Revenge of the Musketeers" (1993), and for his directorial efforts "The Other Side of the Tracks" (1997), "Etoiles: Dancers of the Paris Opera Ballet" (2001), and "Les enfants de Thiès" (2001). He is the son of film director Bertrand Tavernier.

Round Midnight (film) | Round Midnight is a 1986 American-French musical drama film directed by Bertrand Tavernier and written by Tavernier and David Rayfiel. It stars Dexter Gordon, François Cluzet and Herbie Hancock. Martin Scorsese, Philippe Noiret and Wayne Shorter appear in cameos.
source_state.summary_2: Bertrand Tavernier is a French director, screenwriter, actor and producer; no supporting evidence in the provided passages identifies Charles Giblyn’s occupations, so their mutual occupations are unclear.

output.query: Charles Giblyn Bertrand Tavernier and Charles Giblyn, have which mutual occupations?

retrieved_doc_snippets: Charles Giblyn | Charles Giblyn (September 6, 1871 – March 14, 1934) was an American film director and actor of the silent era. He directed 96 films between 1912 and 1927. He also appeared in 23 films between 1914 and 1934. He was one of the founders of the Motion Picture Directors Association. Founded Albion Productions, a film production company, in 1922. He was born in Watertown, New York and died in Los Angeles, California.

The Oubliette | The Oubliette is a 1914 American silent drama film directed by Charles Giblyn, featuring Murdock MacQuarrie, Pauline Bush, and Lon Chaney. It is part of a four-film series directed by Giblyn called the "The Adventures of François Villon". "The Oubliette" is based on a short story of the same name by George Bronson Howard published in "The Century Magazine". This film and "By the Sun's Rays" are two of Lon Chaney's earliest surviving films.

The Price of a Party | The Price of a Party is a 1924 American silent melodrama film produced by Howard Estabrook and distributed by Associated Exhibitors. The film is based on a short story of the same name by William Briggs MacHarg with the film's scenario written by Charles F. Roebuck. It was directed by Charles Giblyn and stars Hope Hampton and Harrison Ford.

Just for Tonight (film) | Just for Tonight is a surviving 1918 silent film comedy-drama directed by Charles Giblyn and starring Tom Moore. It was produced and distributed by Goldwyn Pictures, one of the predecessors of MGM.

The Higher Law (1914 film) | The Higher Law is a 1914 American silent drama film directed by Charles Giblyn and featuring Murdock MacQuarrie, Pauline Bush and Lon Chaney. The film is now considered to be lost.

Know Your Men | Know Your Men is a 1921 American silent melodrama film produced and distributed by Fox Film Corporation, directed by Charles Giblyn, and starring Pearl White. It is now considered to be a lost film.

Sunshine Nan | Sunshine Nan is a surviving 1918 American silent comedy drama film starring Ann Pennington and directed by Charles Giblyn. It is based on the novel "Calvary Alley" by Alice Hegan Rice. It was produced by Famous Players-Lasky and distributed by Paramount Pictures.
target_state.summary_2: Bertrand Tavernier and Charles Giblyn are both directors and actors.
